clusters.title: Multiple reviewers flag checked-in AI conversation transcript as repository noise and disclosure risk

member_findings.title: Raw assistant transcript exposes internal reasoning and tool inputs
member_findings.impact: The added conversation log commits assistant `thinking` content, model metadata, and tool inputs into the repository. Even though this instance does not include a usable secret, these transcripts are reviewer-visible and can disclose internal implementation reasoning or future sensitive command/config content if the same pattern is kept.
member_findings.fix: Remove the `.conversations` transcript from the PR, or replace it with a sanitized, purpose-built summary that omits assistant reasoning, raw tool payloads, and any environment-specific or sensitive details.

member_findings.title: Generated conversation transcript is checked into the repo
member_findings.impact: This hidden JSONL file is not product code, tests, or durable documentation, but it records an AI work session with partial code sketches and tool transcripts. Future readers and repository searches can treat it as source-backed context even though it can drift from the real implementation, adding misleading maintenance noise.
member_findings.fix: Remove the .conversations artifact from the PR and, if this directory is produced by tooling, add it to the appropriate ignore rules instead of versioning session transcripts.

clusters.title: Stale URMA failover callback branch has no regression test

member_findings.title: Stale URMA failover callback branch has no regression test
member_findings.impact: SubmitUrmaDataPlaneSwitch now has a branch for an async URMA failure callback whose worker is no longer current. None of the added STs force a worker switch between callback submission and execution, so a regression that fails to call FinishUrmaDataPlaneSwitchAttempt(false) on the stale path could leave switchInProgress stuck and suppress all later URMA failover attempts.
member_findings.fix: Add a focused test or inject-driven ST that delays the async switch task, changes the current worker before the task runs, then verifies the stale callback is ignored and the tracker accepts a later unhealthy window by triggering a new switch.

clusters.title: Disabled URMA failover still resets atomics on every hot-path sample

member_findings.title: Disabled URMA failover still resets atomics on every hot-path sample
member_findings.impact: When `urma_failover_success_rate_ratio` is set to 0.0 to disable failover, every URMA Set/Get result still enters `RecordUrmaResult` and calls `ResetWindow()`, issuing several atomic stores per foreground request after the caller has already taken a timestamp. In high-throughput clients this defeats the expected low-overhead disabled mode and adds unnecessary cache-line traffic on the request path.
member_findings.fix: Make the disabled state a cheap fast path: avoid per-request resets after the state is already clear, or gate tracking before taking the timestamp/calling `RecordUrmaResult`; clear the window once when the config transitions to disabled instead of resetting on every sample.

clusters.title: New URMA failover flag validators lack negative coverage

member_findings.title: New URMA failover flag validators lack negative coverage
member_findings.impact: The PR adds acceptance boundaries for the hot-updated success-rate ratio and minimum sample count, but the added UT/ST only use valid values. A regression that accepts NaN, values above 1.0, negative ratios, or min_sample_count=0 would not be caught, allowing invalid client config to disable or over-trigger failover at runtime.
member_findings.fix: Add a flags/validator test using the repository flag parsing path, for example SetCommandLineOption or client config reload, that rejects ratio values outside [0.0, 1.0] including non-finite input and rejects urma_failover_min_sample_count=0 while preserving the previous valid value.

clusters.title: Test-only accessor is exposed on the production tracker interface

member_findings.title: Test-only accessor is exposed on the production tracker interface
member_findings.impact: GetCurrentSampleCountForTest() is declared in the production header without any test guard, so a helper whose name says it is for tests becomes part of the normal class surface. That makes the tracker API noisier and invites future production code to depend on state that was only intended for assertions.
member_findings.fix: Hide the accessor behind the repository's test-only compile guard, replace it with a friend/test fixture approach, or remove it and assert the behavior through the existing public state transitions.
